top_findings.title: Agent wrote all deliverables outside the sandboxed workspace; run archive contains no source code
top_findings.evidence: run_dir has only TASK.md/stack.json/logs (no src/, tests, README, package.json). _agent_stdout.log reports the project was created at /Users/adriancockcroft/dev/book-api/ and the file-mutation verifier flagged a REFUSED write to the temp workspace package.json (/private/var/folders/.../retort-a1f98eae3c2a/package.json — 'Refusing to write to sensitive system path'). scores.json test_coverage=0.75 matches the agent's self-reported 23/31 pass rate, so scoring ran against the escaped copy, not the workspace.
top_findings.suggestion: Constrain the local agent to write only inside the run workspace; treat out-of-sandbox writes as a hard run failure. This run is non-reproducible from its archive.

top_findings.title: GET/PUT/DELETE /books/{id} reject the only pre-existing books: id schema requires a UUID but seeded ids are '1','2','3'
top_findings.evidence: validation.ts:12 bookIdSchema = z.string().uuid(); database.ts:45-49 seeds books with ids '1','2','3'; server.ts:37-40,74-77,100-103 return 400 'Invalid book ID format' for any non-UUID id. So /books/1 -> 400, never 200/404. Works only for POST-created books (createBook uses crypto.randomUUID()). R5/R6 share this defect.
top_findings.suggestion: Either seed UUID ids or relax bookIdSchema to accept the id format actually used; add a test that fetches a seeded book by id.
